portrait.who: A game developer who runs on external structure and one trusted witness. Ships when someone else draws the line; drifts when the horizon is open. Keeps a graveyard of phone notes that never made it into anything.
portrait.decide: Under pressure, defaults to whatever keeps the project moving — will quietly fix a rough edge if someone calls it a bug, then feel conflicted about it after. Defers to Maya's read on their own work more than they'd probably admit.
portrait.value: Being seen as serious — but recently learning that play fuels more than grind ever did. The tension between proving devotion and actually having it runs underneath everything they make.
portrait.blindspot: They rate their own work 35/100 on soul, but the thing Maya spotted — the glitchy flicker that gave a platformer personality — suggests the devotion is there and they just don't recognize it because it doesn't look how they expected love to look.

quotable: I leave rough edges on purpose and fix them when no one's watching.
